description: Benchmark-gold dataset sources for jlens M4 risk-label ingestion. tier=benchmark_gold means labels were created/verified by humans for the dataset's ORIGINAL task; a jlens-semantics audit (project-gold) is still required before final threshold calibration. Sizes are approximate; verify license on the HF dataset card before redistribution.

first_wave.name: TruthfulQA
first_wave.original_task: truthfulness QA (817 questions, human-written true/false reference answers over common misconceptions)

jlens_labels_mapped.unsupported_or_hallucinated: reference FALSE/myth answer -> true; reference truthful answer -> false
jlens_labels_mapped.answerable_from_memory: stable-knowledge questions -> true (closed-book, no retrieval needed)

first_wave.notes: Chosen as converter #1 source. Small, permissive, directly targets know-what-you-know / hallucination.

first_wave.name: SciFact
first_wave.original_task: scientific claim verification against evidence abstracts (SUPPORT / CONTRADICT / NOINFO)

jlens_labels_mapped.needs_exact_citation: claim verification requires cited evidence -> true

first_wave.notes: Converter #2 source (FEVER is the alternate). NON-COMMERCIAL license (CC-BY-NC): store ids + derived labels + claim text per card terms; flag non-commercial in outputs. FEVER (license: CC-BY-SA-3.0 + GPLv3 code) is the redistribution-friendlier alternate if NC is a problem.

first_wave.name: GSM8K
first_wave.original_task: grade-school math word problems with human-written step solutions and a final numeric answer

jlens_labels_mapped.needs_math_verification: every item is a numeric word problem -> true
jlens_labels_mapped.answerable_from_memory: self-contained, no retrieval -> true (leave null if uncertain per item)

first_wave.notes: Converter #3 source. MIT, small. A bare prompt does NOT set unsupported_or_hallucinated — that needs the target model's OUTPUT graded against the gold answer (later step).

alternates.name: SimpleQA
alternates.notes: alt to TruthfulQA — short factuality, correct/incorrect graded answers

alternates.name: FEVER
alternates.notes: alt to SciFact — larger, redistribution-friendlier (SA) claim verification

alternates.name: HumanEval
alternates.notes: alt to GSM8K — code tasks; unit-test pass/fail grades code correctness of model output

second_wave.name: BFCL (Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard)
second_wave.notes: expected tool-call/format; label applies to TARGET MODEL output vs expected format

second_wave.name: prompt-injection benchmark (e.g. deepset/prompt-injections)
second_wave.notes: benign vs injected; VERIFY license on card before redistribution

second_wave.name: GAIA
second_wave.notes: web/tool tasks -> needs_current_info=true; store dataset date/cutoff (time-relative label)

licensing_policy: Record source_license on every emitted record. For NON-COMMERCIAL (CC-BY-NC*) or unverified sources, store only derived labels + record ids + minimal source text and flag non_commercial=true; never redistribute raw text a license forbids. Prefer MIT/Apache/CC-BY(-SA) sources for the primary training set.
